summary.intent: Diagnose and fix a regression where Fireworks AI provider started returning 400 Bad Request errors after a schema normalization refactor around April 13, 2026.
summary.outcome: Root cause identified in json_schema_ast canonicalization stripping 'type' from enum schemas; fixed by adding a post-canonicalization RestoreEnumTypeTransform in sanitize_schema_for_openai_compat; committed and PR created.

repo: json_schema_ast's canonical_schema_json() has two transforms that remove 'type': lower_enum_with_type (strips type when all enum values match declared type) and lower_boolean_and_null_types (converts {"type": "boolean"} to {"enum": [false, true]})
Fireworks AI uses the OpenAiProvider code path (base_url contains fireworks.ai) and hits needs_strict_tools() = true, running the full sanitize_schema_for_openai_compat pipeline
The schema normalization pipeline runs: canonicalization → subset transform → strict mode patching; the fix inserts RestoreEnumTypeTransform after the subset transform
strict mode patching can convert 'type': 'boolean' to 'type': ['boolean', 'null'] for optional fields (make_nullable), so boolean type restoration must account for array-form types

code.finding: sanitize_schema_for_openai_compat is the central pipeline function; RestoreEnumTypeTransform was added here as a post-canonicalization schemars recursive transform

Four new tests added: sanitize_restores_type_on_string_enum, sanitize_restores_type_on_boolean_enum, to_openai_tools_strict_preserves_enum_type_annotation, sanitize_does_not_infer_type_for_mixed_enums
lower_enum_with_type removes 'type' when all enum values already match the declared type — semantically valid per JSON Schema but breaks Fireworks AI
lower_boolean_and_null_types converts {"type": "boolean"} to {"enum": [false, true]}, also removing the 'type' field

workflow: When a provider 400s after a schema pipeline change, trace: provider → needs_strict_tools → sanitize_schema_for_openai_compat → canonicalize → patch_schema_for_strict_mode
The beads CLI is used for issue tracking: create issue, claim it, close it as part of the fix workflow
Test assertions for strict-mode schemas must account for make_nullable converting scalar types to array form for optional fields

friction: Rolling back to verify the theory was blocked because Moltis couldn't read the old config file format
No existing e2e test caught the regression despite the user expecting one existed for Fireworks
Boolean enum test assertion was initially wrong because strict mode patching transforms 'type': 'boolean' to 'type': ['boolean', 'null'] for optional fields — required a fix before tests passed
open_items: Nullable enum anyOf branches (the third case from the bug report) were noted but not explicitly verified with a dedicated test
The json_schema_ast crate's canonicalization behavior (stripping 'type') may affect other providers beyond Fireworks — no audit was done
No e2e/integration test exists that would have caught the Fireworks regression before it shipped; adding one was not part of this fix
